title: Fyers WebSocket adapter (primary live source)
acceptance_criteria: FIRST: verify Bun-compat spike — add a comment at the top of fyers.ts documenting the outcome of: (1) import 'fyers-api-v3' in a test script under Bun; (2) confirm WebSocket connects to Fyers sandbox or that the SDK's WS client initialises without error. If incompatibility is found, document the workaround used.

src/types/fyers-api-v3.d.ts declares module 'fyers-api-v3' covering: fyersDataSocket class with constructor({ symbol, accessToken, appId, onmessage, onerror, onclose }), .connect(), .subscribe(symbols: string[]), .unsubscribe(symbols: string[]), .disconnect(). Also covers fyersModel (or equivalent auth class) if used
src/ingestion/brokers/fyers.ts exports class FyersBrokerFeed implements BrokerFeed
Constructor takes { appId: string, accessToken: string } — reads from FYERS_APP_ID and FYERS_ACCESS_TOKEN env vars if not provided
connect(): initialises the fyersDataSocket, wires onmessage/onerror/onclose handlers, calls .connect()

CRITICAL — disconnect type detection: on close/error, inspect the error/reason: if it contains 'invalid token', 'unauthorized', 'authentication', or HTTP 401 → classify as DisconnectReason.AUTH_FAILURE; emit 'disconnect' with reason AUTH_FAILURE; log ERROR: 'Fyers auth failure — access token may be expired. Regenerate before next market open.' Do NOT retry on AUTH_FAILURE.

CRITICAL — transient reconnect: for any other disconnect → emit 'reconnecting' event with attempt number; reconnect with exponential backoff (2s, 4s, 8s, 16s, 32s… capped at 60s); add ±25% jitter; NO maximum retry count for transient disconnects — keep retrying indefinitely until connect() succeeds or disconnect() is called manually
subscribe(symbols): calls the underlying SDK subscribe with the symbol list; re-subscribes after reconnect
Each incoming tick mapped to BrokerTick format; raw message parsed defensively (try/catch around JSON.parse with logging on error)
FYERS_ACCESS_TOKEN and FYERS_APP_ID are NEVER logged, even at DEBUG level — log only the first 4 chars + '...' if needed for debugging
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of every non-obvious decision
